package repository

import (
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
	"testing"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

// Anti-pattern: Repository tests depend on HTTP transport types like
// *gin.Context and http.ResponseWriter instead of testing pure repository behavior.

type Tag struct {
	ID   string `gorm:"column:id"`
	Name string `gorm:"column:name"`
}

type TagRepository struct {
	db *gorm.DB
}

func (r *TagRepository) FindByID(id string) (*Tag, error) {
	var tag Tag
	err := r.db.First(&tag, "id = ?", id).Error
	return &tag, err
}

func (r *TagRepository) Create(tag *Tag) error {
	return r.db.Create(tag).Error
}

// TestFindByID uses *gin.Context in a repository test - anti-pattern.
func TestFindByID(t *testing.T) {
	gin.SetMode(gin.TestMode)
	w := httptest.NewRecorder()
	c, _ := gin.CreateTestContext(w)

	repo := &TagRepository{db: nil}
	c.Set("repo", repo)

	tag, err := repo.FindByID("tag-1")
	if err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusNotFound, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		t.FailNow()
	}

	c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"data": tag})

	if w.Code != http.StatusOK {
		t.Errorf("expected status 200, got %d", w.Code)
	}
}

// TestCreate uses http.ResponseWriter in a repository test - anti-pattern.
func TestCreate(t *testing.T) {
	w := httptest.NewRecorder()

	repo := &TagRepository{db: nil}
	tag := &Tag{ID: "tag-2", Name: "golang"}

	err := repo.Create(tag)
	if err != nil {
		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)
		t.FailNow()
	}

	w.WriteHeader(http.StatusCreated)
	if w.Code != http.StatusCreated {
		t.Errorf("expected status 201, got %d", w.Code)
	}
}
